name: Genetic Algorithm (GA)
applicable_scenarios: black-box optimization where gradients are unavailable
combinatorial problems with large discrete search spaces
python_template:
import numpy as np

def ga(fitness, bounds, pop_size=50, n_gen=100, pc=0.9, pm=0.1, rng=None):
    rng = rng or np.random.default_rng(0)
    lo, hi = np.asarray(bounds).T
    pop = rng.uniform(lo, hi, size=(pop_size, len(lo)))
    for _ in range(n_gen):
        scores = np.array([fitness(ind) for ind in pop])
        idx = np.argsort(scores)
        pop = pop[idx]
        children = []
        while len(children) < pop_size:
            a, b = pop[rng.integers(pop_size // 2, size=2)]
            c = np.where(rng.random(len(lo)) < pc, a, b)
            mask = rng.random(len(lo)) < pm
            c = np.where(mask, rng.uniform(lo, hi), c)
            children.append(c)
        pop = np.array(children)
    scores = np.array([fitness(ind) for ind in pop])
    return pop[np.argmin(scores)]
typical_cases: hyper-parameter search when evaluations are cheap
common_pitfalls: no convergence guarantees; stagnation on deceptive landscapes
heavy dependence on operator / parameter tuning
slow when fitness evaluation is expensive; cache or surrogate
elitism without diversity control loses exploration quickly
